reason: Modifying the mount options for the /boot partition directly affects the system's ability to mount the boot filesystem during startup. An incorrect fstab entry can prevent the kernel and initramfs from loading, resulting in an unbootable system. Because this is a critical filesystem change, it is classified as too dangerous to remediate automatically.
estimated_impact: reboot required; filesystems affected; Potential boot failure; Inability to load kernel/initramfs; System downtime if misconfigured
